subject: fix(a11y): restore WCAG 2.5.5 touch targets + theme-aware skip link
body:
The DaisyUI migration inadvertently regressed several mobile touch targets
and broke the keyboard skip-to-content link's theme adaptation. All caught
by the post-migration a11y/mobile/dark-mode audit.

- src/index.css: .skip-to-content reads `var(--color-primary)` +
  `var(--color-primary-content)` instead of hardcoded #1e293b / #fff. DaisyUI
  exposes these per theme, so the focused skip pill now has high contrast
  against both corporate and business page surfaces (it was near-invisible
  on business's #202020 page bg before).
- NotificationBell.tsx: "Mark all read" btn-xs (24-28px) → btn-sm +
  min-h-[44px]. Delete icon btn-sm btn-square (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px]. Both failed WCAG 2.5.5.
- PwaInstallPrompt.tsx: Install / How-to-Install CTAs swap from naked
  `btn btn-sm` (32px) to `<Button size="sm">` (40px with the wrapper's
  WCAG override). Dismiss btn-sm btn-circle (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px].
- Input.tsx: every form input gets `min-h-[44px] text-base`. DaisyUI's
  input-bordered defaults to 40px / 14px — iOS Safari auto-zooms on focus
  when font-size < 16px. text-base (16px) + 44px height fixes both.
- TextArea.tsx: text-base added (no height override — textareas are
  multi-row by nature).
- ProposeChangeForm.tsx + ModuleEditor.tsx content textareas + AddNoteForm
  note textarea: text-base added on the direct DaisyUI `textarea
  textarea-bordered` usage (these bypass the TextArea wrapper).
- AddNoteForm.tsx source select: min-h-[44px] text-base on
  `select select-bordered`.

Net: every primary form input on mobile now prevents iOS auto-zoom + meets
WCAG 2.5.5; every icon button in the migrated code path now hits 44×44.

Tags: fix, a11y, mobile, wcag, ios-safari
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: patch
